title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Guidance Claude Code generating, editing, extending R package.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: centrcanon open-source R package provides analytical engine CEnTR*MAP — Shiny-based workflow supporting institutions communities collaboratively constructing local definition community-engaged research (CEnR).

what: The end product is a definition, not just a map
title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: package produce definition directly. produces scores help practitioners identify concepts central, structurally connected, influential institution’s conceptual landscape. scores used — faculty, community partners, working groups — organize, prioritize, synthesize terms locally-grounded, community-constructed definition CEnR. words: map means, end. three pipelines surface underlying structure community scholars talks work, structure can inform language ultimately adopt.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Together, high scores across three pipelines identify strongest candidates inclusion local CEnR definition. Clusters (PAM) communities (network detection) suggest terms can grouped coherent themes commitments within definition. Shiny application calls package closed-source paywalled. package must remain completely standalone — Shiny imports, UI code, app-specific configuration. functions must work R console.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Follow Tidyverse Style Guide throughout. Key rules: snake_case function names, argument names, variables. Informative verb-noun names: calculate_anchoring_score(), create_network(), classify_node_role(). Avoid generic names like process() run(). One function per file good default; group tightly related small helpers together (e.g., utils-rank.R). lines 80 characters. Wrap operator pipe end preceding line. Use native pipe |> (R ≥ 4.1), %>%, unless package-specific pipe unavoidable. library() require() calls inside package functions. Use package::function() non-imported calls, declare NAMESPACE via @importFrom. @param, @return, @examples, @export roxygen tags required every exported function. Internal helpers get @noRd @export. Prefer tibble output bare data.frame user-facing functions. Use dplyr/tidyr idioms data manipulation; avoid base R apply loops unless performance demands . Numeric output columns double; categorical outputs factor explicit levels.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
code: centrcanon/ ├── DESCRIPTION ├── NAMESPACE                  # generated by roxygen2, never edit by hand ├── R/ │   ├── tier.R                 # calculate_tier() │   ├── ca_analysis.R          # prepare_contingency_table(), run_correspondence_analysis() │   ├── pam_clustering.R       # run_pam_clustering() │   ├── anchoring.R            # calculate_anchoring_score() │   ├── network.R              # create_network(), calculate_network_metrics() │   ├── integration.R          # calculate_integration_score() │   ├── leverage.R             # classify_node_role(), calculate_leverage_score() │   ├── palettes.R             # earthtone_palette(), centr_palette()  [unexported constants] │   └── utils-rank.R           # dense_rank_normalize(), minmax_scale(), calculate_entropy() ├── tests/ │   └── testthat/ │       ├── test-tier.R │       ├── test-anchoring.R │       ├── test-integration.R │       └── test-leverage.R ├── vignettes/ │   └── centr-pipeline.Rmd     # end-to-end walkthrough ├── man/                       # generated by roxygen2 ├── data-raw/                  # scripts that produce any bundled test data └── inst/     └── extdata/               # small example CSV for vignette / tests

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
code: # Min-max normalize a numeric vector; returns 0.5 for constant input minmax_scale(x)  -> double vector in [0, 1]  # Shannon entropy normalized by log(n), where n = length(values) calculate_entropy(values)  -> double scalar in [0, 1]  # Dense-rank then normalize to [0, 1] dense_rank_normalize(x)  -> double vector in [0, 1]

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Cuts scores 40th, 60th, 80th percentiles. Returns ordered factor levels "Low" < "Medium" < "High" < "High". Must handle NA values via na.rm = TRUE internal quantile() calls.
code: calculate_tier(scores)

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Step 0 — Prepare contingency table Input: tibble columns group, , (caller already renamed raw source columns, e.g. hex1/hex2, /). Pivots single descriptor column endpoints edge treated concept descriptors co-occurring group. Filters NA empty-string descriptors. Cross-tabulates group × descriptor via table(), removes zero-sum rows columns. Returns matrix suitable passing directly run_correspondence_analysis(). Step 1 — Correspondence analysis Step 2 — PAM clustering CA coordinates Default k = 6L; caller may override. Clusters dimensions 1–2 ca_result$col$coord. Step 3 — Anchoring score Returns tibble columns: exemplar sub-score logic: - cluster, compute cluster centroid apply(coords, 2, median). - Distance centroid = Euclidean; invert weight cos2_total (sum cos2 across dims 1 & 2). - Single-member clusters get cos2_total directly exemplar score. three sub-scores equally weighted (boosting parameters default exported function; leave clearly documented argument stub needed future extension).
code: prepare_contingency_table(edgelist)  -> matrix (groups × descriptors) run_correspondence_analysis(contingency_table)  -> FactoMineR::CA object run_pam_clustering(ca_result, k = 6L)  -> cluster::pam object calculate_anchoring_score(ca_result, cluster_assignments)

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Network construction Input: two-column data frame / tibble columns (lowercase; document rename source /). Calls igraph::simplify() remove loops multi-edges. Returns undirected graph. Network metrics Returns tibble one row per node columns: centiserve functions wrapped tryCatch; failure, fill column NA_real_ emit rlang::warn(). graphs vcount(g) <= 1, skip community detection set community = factor(1). Integration score Returns input tibble augmented :
code: create_network(edgelist)  -> igraph::igraph object calculate_network_metrics(graph) calculate_integration_score(network_metrics_tbl)

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Leverage score + node classification Uses eigen, katz, cbet columns (calculate_network_metrics()). Returns input tibble augmented : Node role classification Internal helper (@noRd). Classification logic (evaluated order): three ≥ q90 → "Core Keystone" three ≥ q75 entropy ≥ 0.9 → "Supporting Keystone" eigen + katz high → "Beacon" eigen + cbet high → "Steward" cbet + katz high → "Aqueduct" else → "Hybrid" three ≤ q25 → "Non-Key" Exactly one ≥ q75 → "Sage" (eigen), "Weaver" (cbet), "Messenger" (katz) Two ≥ q75 → paired logic (3) else → "Leaning" Quantile thresholds (q25, q75, q90) must computed nodes dataset classified, hardcoded. Pass arguments internal helper tests can inject known values. Node role levels (factor): "Core Keystone", "Supporting Keystone", "Beacon", "Steward", "Aqueduct", "Hybrid", "Sage", "Weaver", "Messenger", "Leaning", "Non-Key"
code: calculate_leverage_score(network_metrics_tbl) classify_node_role(eigen_z, cbet_z, katz_z)  -> character scalar

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Imports (hard dependencies — list DESCRIPTION Imports:): dplyr, tidyr, tibble, rlang, FactoMineR, cluster, igraph Suggests (soft dependencies — list DESCRIPTION Suggests:): centiserve, expm, testthat (>= 3.0.0), knitr, rmarkdown, ggplot2, ggrepel, ggraph centiserve Suggests, Imports, unavailable CRAN. calls centiserve functions must guarded:
code: Package: centrcanon Title: Analytical Engine for CEnTR*MAP Community-Engaged Research Mapping Version: 0.1.0 License: MIT + file LICENSE Encoding: UTF-8 Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) RoxygenNote: 7.x.x if (requireNamespace("centiserve", quietly = TRUE)) {   centiserve::crossclique(g) } else {   rlang::warn(     "centiserve is not installed; `crossclique` set to NA. \\      Install from GitHub: muuankarski/centiserve"   )   rep(NA_real_, igraph::vcount(g)) }

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Use testthat (3rd edition). Declare Config/testthat/edition: 3 DESCRIPTION. test file mirrors source file. Minimum coverage expectations: test-tier.R: boundary values, NA handling, correct factor levels ordering. test-anchoring.R: prepare_contingency_table() produces correct dimensions drops zero-sum rows/cols; score [0, 1]; single-concept cluster handled; factor levels correct; calculate_tier() output attached. test-integration.R: score [0, 1]; disconnected graph returns valid result; centiserve-absent path returns NA columns warning. test-leverage.R: classify_node_role() branch reachable synthetic 3-node case; leverage_score [0, 1]. Build minimal synthetic fixtures tests/testthat/helper-fixtures.R rather loading external CSV files.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: package raw data loading — belongs calling app. source CSV columns group, hex1, hex2; caller renames hex1/hex2 /passing edgelist package function. single prepared input package works : edgelist, two analytical inputs derived: - Contingency table — via prepare_contingency_table(edgelist), package provides. treated descriptors co-occurring group; result group × descriptor matrix. - Network graph — via create_network(edgelist), treats /directed node pairs simplifies undirected graph. Document clearly vignette minimal worked example showing rename step downstream calls. small anonymized example dataset can live inst/extdata/ use tests vignette. Cluster IDs returned run_pam_clustering() integers; human-readable cluster labels caller’s responsibility must hardcoded package.

what: What this package must NOT do
title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Import depend shiny, flexdashboard, UI framework. Produce flextable officer output — table formatting belongs app. Call set.seed() — reproducibility caller’s responsibility. Document vignette. Use print(), cat(), message() normal output — use rlang::inform() optional diagnostics , keep behind verbose = FALSE argument. Hardcode file paths dataset-specific column names inside functions.

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Follow Semantic Versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH. Maintain NEWS.md section per release format used usethis::use_news_md().

title: CLAUDE.md — centrcanon
text: Always build incrementally, phase phase. Never write files run make check one shot — errors become hard untangle. phase covers coherent slice package. Complete phase starting next: write edit relevant R/ file(s), run phase target document test slice. Rules: - Run make document every change roxygen tags exported function signatures, running tests. - Run phase-specific test target (e.g. make test-anchoring) developing single file. Run make test phase complete. - proceed next phase current phase’s tests pass make document produces warnings. - Run make check five phases green. Resolve every NOTE, WARNING, ERROR committing. - one acceptable NOTE "New submission" first CRAN submission. First-time setup:
code: make setup      # install declared dependencies make document   # generate NAMESPACE and man/ make phase1     # verify the foundation before writing anything else

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: centrcanon provides analytical engine CEnTR*MAP, workflow supports institutions communities collaboratively constructing local definition community-engaged research (CEnR). package produce definition. produces scores surface underlying structure community scholars talks work — identifying terms central, best connected, influential. scores guide practitioners organizing prioritizing terms locally-grounded, community-constructed definition. three analytical pipelines answer different question structure: three pipelines share single prepared input: edgelist tibble.

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: source CSV uses columns group, hex1, hex2, hex1 hex2 two concept descriptor codes associated response. Rename columns passing package function. centrcanon step. Note reproducibility: centrcanon never calls set.seed(). need reproducible PAM community-detection results, call set.seed() script running pipeline.
code: library(readr) library(dplyr) library(centrcanon)  raw <- read_csv("path/to/data.csv")  edgelist <- raw |>   rename(from = hex1, to = hex2) |>          # rename raw columns   select(group, from, to) |>   filter(!is.na(from), !is.na(to))

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: anchoring pipeline uses correspondence analysis (CA) find dimensional structure concept space, PAM clustering group concepts, finally scores concept salience, dimensional definition, cluster exemplar proximity. Cluster IDs anchoring$cluster integers. Human-readable labels (e.g. “Methodological Foundations”) caller’s responsibility.
code: # Step 0: build the group × descriptor contingency table ct <- prepare_contingency_table(edgelist)  # Step 1: correspondence analysis ca <- run_correspondence_analysis(ct)  # Step 2: PAM clustering on CA dimensions 1–2 (default k = 6) pam <- run_pam_clustering(ca, k = 6L)  # Step 3: anchoring scores anchoring <- calculate_anchoring_score(ca, pam$clustering) anchoring

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: integration pipeline treats concept pairs network edges scores node structural role connector within network. crossclique, cflow, cbet, katz require centiserve package (CRAN). installed, columns NA warning. Install :
code: # Build the network g <- create_network(edgelist)  # Compute centrality metrics metrics <- calculate_network_metrics(g)  # Integration scores integration <- calculate_integration_score(metrics) integration remotes::install_github("muuankarski/centiserve")

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: leverage pipeline classifies node one eleven roles based eigenvector, communicability betweenness, Katz centrality compare rest network. Role levels (ordered reference): "Core Keystone", "Supporting Keystone", "Beacon", "Steward", "Aqueduct", "Hybrid", "Sage", "Weaver", "Messenger", "Leaning", "Non-Key".
code: leverage <- calculate_leverage_score(metrics) leverage |> select(name, node_role, leverage_score, tier)

title: The CEnTR*MAP Analytical Pipeline
text: Two palettes provided use visualizations (gradient helpers ggplot2 theme code belong calling application):
code: centr_earthtone_palette()  # 8-color earthtone palette centr_layer_palette()      # 3-color palette: anchoring / integration / leverage

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
text: Combines three sub-scores — salience, dimensional definition, cluster exemplar proximity — normalized anchoring score concept descriptor identified correspondence analysis.

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
code: calculate_anchoring_score(ca_result, cluster_assignments)

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
text: ca_result CA object run_correspondence_analysis(). cluster_assignments integer vector PAM cluster IDs, one per concept (.e. pam_result$clustering).

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
text: tibble one row per concept columns: concept character. Concept / descriptor name. cluster integer. PAM cluster ID. salience_raw double. CA column inertia. dimensional_raw double. Sum contributions CA dims 1 & 2. cluster_exemplar_raw double. Proximity-weighted cos2 score. rank_salience integer. Dense rank salience_raw. rank_dimensional integer. Dense rank dimensional_raw. rank_exemplar integer. Dense rank cluster_exemplar_raw. combined_rank double. Mean three ranks. anchoring_score double. Normalized [0, 1]. percentile_rank double. 0–100. anchoring_category ordered factor. "Peripheral Element" < "Supporting Element" < "Secondary Anchor" < "Primary Anchor". tier ordered factor. calculate_tier().

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
text: Sub-scores equally weighted. three dense-ranked, summed, min-max normalized produce anchoring_score.

title: Calculate conceptual anchoring scores — calculate_anchoring_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ct  <- prepare_contingency_table(edgelist) ca  <- run_correspondence_analysis(ct) pam <- run_pam_clustering(ca) calculate_anchoring_score(ca, pam$clustering) } # }

title: Normalized Shannon entropy — calculate_entropy
text: Computes Shannon entropy numeric vector, normalized log(n) n length values. Returns scalar [0, 1]. Returns 0 single-element vectors (entropy undefined; treated minimum).

code: calculate_entropy(values)

title: Normalized Shannon entropy — calculate_entropy
text: values numeric vector non-negative values.

title: Normalized Shannon entropy — calculate_entropy
text: double scalar [0, 1].

title: Normalized Shannon entropy — calculate_entropy
code: calculate_entropy(c(1, 1, 1))    # maximum entropy -> 1 #> [1] 1 calculate_entropy(c(10, 0, 0))   # minimum entropy -> 0 #> [1] 1.55006e-15

title: Calculate integration scores from network metrics — calculate_integration_score
text: input tibble augmented columns: betweenness_scaled double. Min-max scaled betweenness. cflow_scaled double. Min-max scaled current-flow closeness. crossclique_scaled double. Min-max scaled cross-clique. rank_betweenness integer. Dense rank betweenness_scaled. rank_cflow integer. Dense rank cflow_scaled. rank_crossclique integer. Dense rank crossclique_scaled. rank_sum integer. Sum three ranks. integration_score double. Normalized [0, 1]. tier ordered factor. calculate_tier().

what: Calculate leverage scores and classify node roles — calculate_leverage_score
title: Calculate leverage scores and classify node roles — calculate_leverage_score
text: Derives leverage score node combining eigenvector centrality, communicability betweenness (cbet), Katz centrality via dense ranking min-max normalization. node also classified one eleven leverage roles based centrality profile compares rest network.

code: calculate_leverage_score(network_metrics_tbl)

title: Calculate leverage scores and classify node roles — calculate_leverage_score
text: network_metrics_tbl tibble returned calculate_network_metrics(), containing minimum columns eigen, cbet, katz.

title: Calculate leverage scores and classify node roles — calculate_leverage_score
text: input tibble augmented columns: eigen_z double. Min-max scaled eigenvector centrality. katz_z double. Min-max scaled Katz centrality. cbet_z double. Min-max scaled communicability betweenness. rank_eigen integer. Dense rank eigen_z. rank_katz integer. Dense rank katz_z. rank_cbet integer. Dense rank cbet_z. rank_sum integer. Sum three ranks. leverage_score double. Normalized [0, 1]. tier ordered factor. calculate_tier(). node_role factor. One "Core Keystone", "Supporting Keystone", "Beacon", "Steward", "Aqueduct", "Hybrid", "Sage", "Weaver", "Messenger", "Leaning", "Non-Key".

title: Calculate network centrality metrics — calculate_network_metrics
text: tibble one row per node columns: name character. Node name. degree double. Normalized degree centrality. betweenness double. Normalized betweenness centrality. crossclique double. Cross-clique centrality. cflow double. Current-flow closeness centrality. cbet double. Communicability betweenness centrality (normalized). eigen double. Eigenvector centrality. katz double. Katz centrality. community factor. Optimal-modularity community membership.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ el <- tibble::tibble(   from = c("a", "b", "a"),   to   = c("b", "c", "c") ) g  <- create_network(el) calculate_network_metrics(g) } # }

what: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
text: Cuts numeric score vector four ordered tiers using 40th, 60th, 80th percentiles break points.

title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
code: calculate_tier(scores)

title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
text: scores numeric vector scores.

title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
text: ordered factor levels "Low" < "Medium" < "High" < "High".

title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
text: Quantile type 1 (inverse empirical CDF) used break points always actual observed values rather interpolated midpoints. ensures score exactly 40th percentile falls "Medium", "Low".

title: Classify scores into performance tiers — calculate_tier
code: calculate_tier(c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9)) #> [1] Low       Medium    High      Very High Very High #> Levels: Low < Medium < High < Very High

title: centrcanon 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Anchoring pipeline: prepare_contingency_table(), run_correspondence_analysis(), run_pam_clustering(), calculate_anchoring_score(). Integration pipeline: create_network(), calculate_network_metrics(), calculate_integration_score(). Leverage pipeline: calculate_leverage_score(). Shared utilities: calculate_tier(), minmax_scale(), calculate_entropy(), dense_rank_normalize(). Color palettes: centr_earthtone_palette(), centr_layer_palette().
